reason: User challenged whether prior Wave70 masks were actually strictly reviewed. This superseding audit applies stricter overlay-first semantic QA to the original user-supplied image set and the later mf70_nose case.
policy: Generated-output stability remains separate evidence and must not mark mask completion. Shortcut polygons, partial targets, or protected-neighbor overlap fail or require revision even when low-denoise output is stable.

findings: Strict review of the project overlay shows a broad shortcut polygon over the identity triangle that spills into hair/side regions and cannot be certified against the protected-neighbor contract for eyes, nose, mouth, jawline, and hairline.
Replace with a region-aware identity mask or split into protected submasks; do not certify this polygon as anatomically honest face identity coverage.
Project overlay is a broad face polygon, not a region-aware brows/eyes/cheeks/mouth expression mask; it includes identity-sensitive neighbors and hair/side-face context.
Split expression-region coverage into precise brow, eye-adjacent, cheek-motion, and mouth-expression zones with protected teeth/hairline/background exclusions.
Forehead overlay is mostly in the right area but is a shortcut polygon that rides into hairline/brow-adjacent protected neighbors; strict QA should not mark it pass.
Regenerate as a forehead-skin band bounded by hairline and eyebrow clearance, with a small inset from both protected boundaries.
Original side-by-side cheek masks are geometric cheekbone patches, too close to eyes/nose and not faithful left/right cheek-skin coverage.
Use cheek landmarks/contours that cover the cheek surface while excluding eyes, nose, mouth, hairline, and jawline.
Jaw/chin overlay is a V-shaped shortcut under/around the mouth, not a faithful jawline/chin contour, and it risks mouth/lip neighbor intrusion.
Trace the visible chin and jaw contour with explicit mouth, neck, hair, and clothing exclusion margins.

Skin-tone continuity overlay includes a large artificial neck/chest/clothing-shaped triangle and broad face polygon; it violates clothing/background protection and is not a valid visible-skin semantic mask.
Regenerate separate face-skin, neck-skin, and chest-skin masks using skin segmentation, explicitly subtracting clothing and background.
Single-eye overlay is an oval shortcut over/around the eye, including surrounding skin/hair/upper-orbital context rather than the visible eye aperture.
Regenerate from eyelid aperture landmarks and subtract protected iris/pupil/lid/lash/skin subregions according to the target label.
Single-eye overlay is an oval shortcut over/around the eye, including eyelid/skin context rather than a precise visible eye aperture.
Regenerate from eyelid aperture landmarks and subtract protected iris/pupil/lid/lash/skin subregions according to the target label.
Eye ovals do not precisely isolate pupils, irises, and visible sclera; they include eyelid/skin context and do not clearly cover sclera correctly.
Use eye-aperture segmentation with separate pupil/iris/sclera components and subtract eyelids, lashes, catchlights, and skin.
Eyelid overlay is mostly upper-orbital/brow-adjacent polygons and does not represent upper/lower eyelid bands; it intrudes into protected neighboring regions.
Generate narrow upper and lower eyelid bands from lid-fold/aperture landmarks and exclude iris, sclera, eyelashes, broad skin, and brows.
Eyelash overlay is a pair of shortcut strokes near the eyes, not anatomically reliable upper/lower eyelash coverage, and may cross eyelid/eye-aperture protected regions.
Use lash-line edge detection or landmark-following thin masks that trace actual visible lashes while excluding eyelid, iris, skin, and background.
Under-eye crescents are reasonably aligned to under-eye skin and avoid most protected neighbors; pass only for the narrow under_eye_skin label, not for eyelids, eyes, or cheeks.
Keep but require zoomed review that lower lid, eye aperture, broad cheek, and nose are excluded.
Nose overlay covers only part of the full visible nose, misses a meaningful sidewall/half of the nose, and intrudes near protected mouth/philtrum and inner-eye/canthus regions.
Regenerate full_nose_bridge_tip_nostrils mask symmetrically over the visible bridge, sidewalls, tip, alae, and nostrils, with explicit clearance from eyes, cheeks, philtrum, upper lip, and mouth.

better_masking_requirements: Use actual anatomical landmarks/segmentation for face parts; do not use coarse ovals or broad polygons as passable masks for detailed features.
For full-region labels, cover the full visible target, not half or a convenient subset.
Subtract protected neighbors before output proof: eyes, eyelids, mouth, lips, hairline, clothing, background, and skin neighbors as listed by taxonomy.
Require zoomed overlay review of source, overlay, and generated output; generated-output stability cannot override overlay defects.
Split broad masks into smaller named submasks when one polygon would cross protected boundaries.
